summary: CI/tooling PR that worked around unreliable `ninja diff` status handling in the link job. The workflow now preserves the `ninja` build result, streams `ninja diff` output line by line, marks failure when output contains `ERROR`, and exits with the accumulated result. It also corrected GitHub Actions warning annotation syntax in `tools/diff_changes.py`. A large `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` update changed many symbol scopes and local autogenerated labels, but the decomp.dev bot reported `No changes`, so the substantive intent appears to be CI/reporting rather than game-code matching.
what_changed: In `.github/workflows/build.yml`, the `link` matrix branch no longer runs `set -e` followed by bare `ninja diff`; instead it keeps `set +e`, runs `ninja`, stores `$?` in `result`, then runs `ninja diff` through a `while IFS= read -r line` loop.
The workflow echoes every line from `ninja diff` and sets `result=1` if any line contains the substring `ERROR`, then exits with `exit "$result"`.
In `tools/diff_changes.py`, the GitHub Actions warning annotation was fixed from `::warning One or more functions were broken!` to `::warning::One or more functions were broken!`.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` was regenerated or adjusted with many scope changes, including examples where `grLast_UnkStage0_OnLoad`, `grLast_UnkStage0_OnStart`, `grTmario_UnkStage0_OnLoad`, `grTsamus_UnkStage0_OnStart`, and many `itRabbitc_UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll` symbols changed from global to local.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` also contains opposite scope changes for some symbols, notably many `itNokonoko_UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll` entries changing from local to global, plus renumbering of autogenerated local labels such as `@291` to `@289` and `@191`/`@219` to `@190`/`@218`.
The decomp.dev bot comment for GALE01 reported `No changes`, indicating the PR did not produce reported binary diff changes despite the symbol metadata churn.
smart_moves: Preserved the build step's exit code before running the diff step, so a build failure is not accidentally masked by later commands.
Streamed `ninja diff` output back to the log while inspecting it, preserving observability instead of replacing the diff report with a silent grep.

Detected failure from explicit `ERROR` text in `ninja diff` output, which directly addresses the PR title's return-code problem when the command's status alone is not trusted or not propagated as desired.
Fixed the GitHub Actions annotation format to the documented `::warning::message` form, making broken-function reports render correctly in CI logs.
Kept the CI fix small and localized: one workflow hunk plus a one-line reporting-tool correction.
decomp_lessons: Do not rely blindly on `ninja diff` exit status in CI; if a project-specific diff tool emits meaningful `ERROR` lines, preserve output and explicitly convert those diagnostics into a failing job.
When using shell loops and process substitution around diagnostic commands, explicitly manage a `result` variable and `exit` with it to avoid losing failure information.
CI changes for decomp projects should continue printing full diff output, because maintainers need the exact function or section errors to diagnose matching regressions.
GitHub Actions workflow commands require precise syntax; warnings need `::warning::...`, not `::warning ...`, or annotations may not be recognized.

Large `symbols.txt` changes may be metadata/scope churn rather than source-level decomp progress. Pair them with bot diff reports and avoid inferring game-code changes unless the diff report or source edits support it.
naming_conventions: Stage callback names in symbols use forms like `grLast_UnkStage0_OnLoad` and `grTsamus_UnkStage0_OnStart`.
Item motion callback symbols use the pattern `itRabbitc_UnkMotion<N>_Anim`, `itRabbitc_UnkMotion<N>_Phys`, and `itRabbitc_UnkMotion<N>_Coll`.
Nokonoko item motion callbacks follow the same `itNokonoko_UnkMotion<N>_Anim/Phys/Coll` pattern.
Autogenerated local data labels use numeric names like `@289`, `@190`, and `@218`; these can be renumbered by tooling and should not be overinterpreted as semantic renames.
Address-derived unknown item names appear in forms like `it_2E6A_UnkMotion19_Phys` and `it_2F28_UnkMotion0_Anim` when no stronger semantic name is present.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic is evidenced by the PR body or comments.
The relevant matching-adjacent tactic is CI gating: scan `ninja diff` output for `ERROR` and fail the job even if return-code behavior is unsuitable.
The symbol file scope changes show the importance of keeping linker/symbol metadata in sync with generated expectations, but the evidence does not explain the generator or exact cause.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The only issue comment was from decomp-dev[bot]: `Report for GALE01 ... No changes`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `.github/workflows/build.yml` and `ninja diff` to see whether the ERROR-scanning workaround was retained or replaced.
Search for uses of `::warning::One or more functions were broken!` to confirm GitHub Actions annotations render as intended.
Inspect symbol-generation tooling around `scope:local` and `scope:global` to understand why this PR caused broad `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` churn.
Search for later PRs involving `itRabbitc_UnkMotion` or `itNokonoko_UnkMotion` to see whether these unknown motion callbacks were later given semantic names.
